images.copyright: Great Barrier Reef from above, Queensland, Australia (© Francesco Riccardo Iacomino/Getty Images)
images.title: Underwater architecture
images.caption: Great Barrier Reef from above, Queensland, Australia
images.desc: One of the world's greatest natural wonders stretches through the turquoise waters of the Coral Sea off Australia's northeast shoreline. The Great Barrier Reef, running along the coast of Queensland, is the world's largest coral reef system. It is made up of nearly 3,000 individual reefs and hundreds of islands, spanning more than 1,400 miles.

images.copyright: Polar bear mother and cubs playing in Wapusk National Park, Manitoba, Canada (© Hao Jiang/Getty Images)
images.title: A bond that endures
images.caption: Happy Mother's Day!
images.desc: On Mother's Day, we honour the care, patience and protection that shape lives everywhere. In the wild, these qualities often stand out most where survival is hardest. Captured in Wapusk National Park in northern Manitoba, Canada, today's image shows a polar bear mother and her cubs sharing a playful moment on the Arctic tundra.

images.copyright: Skradinski Buk Waterfall in Krka National Park, Croatia (© Amazing Aerial Agency/Adobe Stock)
images.title: The architect of Krka
images.caption: Skradinski Buk Waterfall, Krka National Park, Croatia
images.desc: Water is the architect of Krka National Park in Croatia, shaping emerald pools and layered waterfalls along the Krka River. Today's image highlights Skradinski Buk, the park's most famous waterfall system, where multiple streams spill over travertine terraces in a dense forest near the village of Lozovac.

images.copyright: Sardinian donkey mare and foal, France (© Klein & Hubert/Nature Picture Library)
images.title: More than a hee-haw
images.caption: World Donkey Day
images.desc: World Donkey Day celebrates one of humanity's most quietly capable partners. Donkeys have worked alongside people for thousands of years, prized for their endurance, sharp memory and sure-footed confidence on rugged ground. Often mistaken for stubborn, they are actually careful thinkers—pausing to assess risk rather than rushing into trouble.

images.copyright: Kofa National Wildlife Refuge, Arizona, United States (© Denis Tangney Jr/Getty Images)
images.title: Desert, drawn wide
images.caption: Kofa National Wildlife Refuge, Arizona, United States
images.desc: Stretching across the Sonoran Desert in southwestern Arizona, United States, Kofa National Wildlife Refuge is a land of sharp peaks, wide skies and surprising life. The refuge was established in 1939 to protect desert bighorn sheep, whose sure-footed silhouettes still define the rugged landscape.

images.title: Plains under pressure
images.caption: Thunderstorm above the plains, Bulgaria
images.desc: Located on the eastern Balkan Peninsula, Bulgaria sits at the crossroads of Europe and Asia, where ancient cultures, fertile landscapes and shifting climates converge.

images.copyright: Tŵr Mawr Lighthouse, Ynys Llanddwyn, Anglesey, Wales (© Lukas Bischoff/Getty Images)
images.title: Light at the end of the isle
images.caption: Tŵr Mawr Lighthouse, Anglesey, Wales
images.desc: At the far edge of Ynys Llanddwyn, where the land quietly gives way to sea, Tŵr Mawr Lighthouse stands watch without making a fuss. It is not tall, not flashy, but it knows its purpose—and it keeps it bright.

images.copyright: Ksar Ouled Soltane, Tataouine district in southern Tunisia (© Dark_Eni/Getty Images Plus)
images.title: Sci‑fi, Earth‑made
images.caption: Star Wars Day
images.desc: May the 4th be with you! On Star Wars Day, reality cosplays as science fiction and dares you to tell the difference. Our image takes us to Ksar Ouled Soltane in Tunisia's Tataouine district, where stacked clay granaries rise like sand‑coloured fortresses. Dating back to the 15th century, this storehouse complex was built by Berber communities to protect harvests from heat, theft and time. Function first. Beauty as a side effect.
